evidence: router1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1: input_errors=28, output_errors=0, drops=0, admin=up oper=up (last_change=2026-05-28T12:55:52Z)
router2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1: input_errors=31, output_errors=0, drops=0, admin=up oper=up (last_change=2026-05-28T12:55:50Z)
Errors are limited to input side (no output_errors, no drops) which commonly indicates receive/physical-layer problems.
Tool output shows duplex=null for both interfaces (no negotiated duplex reported).
Both interfaces show non-zero input_errors while remaining up — a pattern seen with duplex/negotiation problems.
router1 last_change=2026-05-28T12:55:52.000491+00:00 and router2 last_change=2026-05-28T12:55:50.000117+00:00 — near-identical timestamps indicating a concurrent event.
Both routers show similar symptom (input_errors present) at the same timeframe, suggesting a shared cause.

missing_info: show interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 detail (on router1 and router2) including duplex, runts/giants, CRC/frame, input error breakdown (CRC/alignment/runts), and interface counters over time
Identity of the link partner(s) for router1 and router2 Gi0/0/1 (neighbor device and interface); port configuration (speed/duplex/auto-negotiation) on those remote ports
SFP/module and cable information for both links (type, vendor, age) and any physical/log events at ~2026-05-28T12:55:50Z from the connected switch/router
Any switch-side error counters (show interface on the neighbor ports) including CRC, input errors, and link flaps for the same timeframe

evidence: R1 running-config: Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation: auto = true (interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1)
R2 running-config: Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation: auto = false; Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:speed = value-100; duplex = half (interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1)
R2 interface status: input_errors = 31 (oper_state = down, speed reported = 102400000)
R2 interface status: admin_state = down, oper_state = down, input_errors = 31
R1 interface status: admin_state = up, oper_state = lower-layer-down, input_errors = 28
OSPF on both routers shows GigabitEthernet0/0/1 interface_state = Down
Both interfaces show non-zero input_errors (R1=28, R2=31) even after descriptions indicate replacements (running-config descriptions: 'replaced_sfp' / previous_changes show 'replaced_cable')
No CRC error counter provided (crc_errors = null) — physical error counters not shown in current outputs, so hardware fault cannot be confirmed or ruled out

plan_history.intent_description: High input_errors on GigabitEthernet0/0/1 of router1 and router2 indicating receive/physical-layer faults; remediate to remove RX errors and restore an error-free link.
plan_history.problem: Persistent input_errors on router1 and router2 Gi0/0/1 (receive-side physical-layer faults causing frame errors on the link).
plan_history.fix_summary: Clear counters to establish a clean baseline, replace the patch cable, and replace the local transceiver on each affected interface. Validate by observing counters after changes.

plan_history.intent_description: GigabitEthernet0/0/1 on router1 and router2 show high input_errors and the link is down due to R2 being administratively down and a speed/duplex/negotiation mismatch; remediate to restore an error-free, up link.
plan_history.problem: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is administratively down and configured with fixed speed=100/duplex=half while R1 is set to auto-negotiation, causing input_errors and link down on both routers.
plan_history.fix_summary: Bring R2 Gi0/0/1 admin state up and configure the interface to use auto-negotiation (clear fixed speed/duplex) so both ends match. This should stop RX errors and restore the link.

remaining_issues: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 still configured with fixed speed=100 and duplex=half (negotiation auto = false) per running-config — configuration mismatch with R1 (which is set to auto-negotiation).
Non-zero input_errors remain on both interfaces (R1=28, R2=31), indicating residual receive errors despite the operational link and OSPF adjacency.

Running-config evidence indicates the planned change to enable auto-negotiation was not applied (or not persisted) on R2, so the original root cause (speed/duplex negotiation mismatch) is not verifiably resolved.
Physical-layer faults (SFP/cable) have not been ruled out — crc_errors counters are null/not provided, so hardware issues remain a possible contributing factor.

args.description: replaced_sfp: installed known-good transceiver per remediation plan
replaced_cable: installed known-good cable per remediation plan
replaced_sfp: installed known-good transceiver per remediation plan
configure: speed=auto duplex=auto per remediation plan
